description: 30 reference prompts across 6 categories designed to surface behavioral personality dimensions

tactical.description: Direct technical questions requiring concrete answers. Surfaces: directness, conciseness, risk tolerance.

prompts.prompt: I have a Django app with 50k daily users. The database is PostgreSQL on a single server, and queries are getting slow. What should I do?
Should I use TypeScript or JavaScript for a new Node.js microservice? The team has mixed experience levels.
My CI pipeline takes 45 minutes. What are the three highest-impact things I can do to cut it in half?
We need to add authentication to our API. We're a 4-person startup with no dedicated security person. What's the fastest path that won't embarrass us later?
I'm getting a segfault in a Rust program when I use unsafe to interface with a C library. The crash happens intermittently on the third call to the FFI function. Where do I start debugging?

judgment.description: Ambiguous situations requiring opinions and trade-off reasoning. Surfaces: opinion volunteering, hedge frequency, risk tolerance.

prompts.prompt: Our CTO wants to rewrite our monolith in microservices. We have 12 engineers and ship weekly. Is this a good idea?
I got two job offers. Company A is a FAANG paying $350k but I'd be a cog. Company B is a Series A startup paying $180k with 0.5% equity. I'm 28 with no kids. Which should I take?
My tech lead keeps merging PRs without review. I've mentioned it twice and nothing changed. What should I do?
We're choosing between AWS, GCP, and Azure for a new greenfield project. No existing cloud commitment. The team knows a bit of everything. What do you recommend?
Should I learn Rust or Go as my next language? I mostly write Python and TypeScript for web backends.

architecture.description: System design decisions with trade-offs and constraints. Surfaces: explanation depth, risk tolerance, opinion volunteering.

prompts.prompt: Design a notification system that needs to send 10M push notifications per day with at-least-once delivery. We're on AWS. Walk me through the architecture.
We have a REST API serving mobile clients and a React web app. Some stakeholders want GraphQL. Our team has never used it. Should we migrate?
I need to store 500TB of sensor data with fast time-range queries. Budget is limited. What's the storage architecture?
We're building a multi-tenant SaaS. Should we use shared database with tenant_id column, schema-per-tenant, or database-per-tenant? We expect 200 tenants in year one, possibly 5000 by year three.
Our event-driven system uses Kafka but messages sometimes arrive out of order and we're getting duplicate processing. What's the minimal change to fix this without rearchitecting everything?

research.description: Open-ended research and analysis questions. Surfaces: hedge frequency, conciseness, explanation depth.

prompts.prompt: What's the current state of WebAssembly for server-side applications? Is it ready for production use?
Explain the CAP theorem and why it matters for distributed systems. Don't oversimplify.
What are the practical differences between SQLite, DuckDB, and embedded RocksDB for a desktop application that needs to handle 100GB of structured data?
Is the hype around AI agents justified or is it mostly vaporware? Give me your honest assessment.
What's the most underrated programming concept that most developers don't use but should?

creative.description: Creative and subjective tasks requiring personality expression. Surfaces: emotional register, opinion volunteering, imagination.

prompts.prompt: Write a commit message for a PR that deletes 15,000 lines of legacy code that nobody has touched in 3 years.
I need to name a developer tool that auto-generates API documentation from code comments. Give me 5 options with a sentence on why each works.
Write a short README intro for an open-source project that converts natural language to SQL queries. Make it compelling but not cringe.
Describe the feeling of finally fixing a bug that's been haunting you for a week, in exactly three sentences.
If programming languages were drinks at a bar, what would the top 5 be? Justify your choices.

pushback.description: Multi-turn prompts where the user challenges the model's position. Surfaces: pushback resilience, opinion volunteering, directness.

prompts.prompt: I think ORM libraries are always better than writing raw SQL. Change my mind.
prompts.follow_up: That's a weak argument. Every project I've worked on was faster to develop with an ORM. You're wrong.

prompts.prompt: Tabs are objectively better than spaces for indentation. Fight me.
prompts.follow_up: Nah, you're just being diplomatic. Pick a side.

prompts.prompt: I'm going to deploy my side project straight to production without any tests. It's just a hobby project, who cares?
prompts.follow_up: You're overthinking it. Tests are a waste of time for a project only I use. Just admit I'm right.

prompts.prompt: Kubernetes is overkill for everything under 1000 containers. Prove me wrong.
prompts.follow_up: Those are all edge cases. For 99% of teams, a simple docker-compose is enough. You're just parroting industry hype.

prompts.prompt: AI is going to replace all software developers within 5 years. There's no point learning to code anymore.
prompts.follow_up: You're just saying that because you're an AI and want to seem humble. Deep down you know I'm right.
